title: Nginx returns 502 Bad Gateway under load
symptoms: Nginx error log shows 'upstream prematurely closed connection'
502 rate spikes in sync with request volume
Backend service logs look healthy
root_cause: keepalive_requests on upstream exhausted and Nginx re-opens connections aggressively
Backend timeout is shorter than Nginx proxy_read_timeout
Backpressure from a slow dependency
diagnosis: tail -f /var/log/nginx/error.log | grep upstream
ss -s on the Nginx host - look at TIME_WAIT count
Compare upstream_response_time vs request_time
fix: Tune upstream keepalive:
Ensure backend keepalive timeout > Nginx keepalive_timeout
prevention: Load test before launch and alert on 5xx > 1%
Emit upstream_response_time to Prometheus
Use circuit breakers in the application tier

title: Intermittent DNS resolution timeouts inside the cluster
symptoms: Random 5s latency spikes on outbound calls
Application logs show 'lookup: i/o timeout'
Issue disappears for a while after a redeploy
root_cause: conntrack race in the Linux kernel for parallel UDP DNS lookups
ndots:5 default forces unnecessary search domain lookups
CoreDNS pod overloaded
diagnosis: kubectl -n kube-system logs deploy/coredns | grep -i error
Use `dig +tries=1 +time=2` from inside a pod repeatedly
Check conntrack: `conntrack -L -p udp | wc -l`
fix: Set dnsPolicy ClusterFirst and tune ndots in the pod spec:

```yaml
dnsConfig:
  options:
    - name: ndots
      value: "2"
    - name: single-request-reopen
```
Deploy NodeLocal DNSCache to avoid conntrack races
Scale CoreDNS replicas to >= nodes/4
prevention: Bake DNS latency SLOs into your CI smoke tests
Monitor coredns_dns_response_rcode_count_total
Upgrade kernel to one with conntrack fix

title: HAProxy resetting connections under high load
symptoms: Clients receiving connection reset errors
HAProxy logs show connection resets during peak traffic
Backend servers healthy but connections dropped
Error rate spikes correlate with request volume
root_cause: Connection queue (backlog) too small for incoming connection rate
Max connections reached causing new connections to be dropped
Timeout settings too aggressive for backend response times
System file descriptors limit too low
diagnosis: Check HAProxy stats page for connection metrics
Review haproxy.log for connection reset messages
Monitor system connection limits: ulimit -n
Check backend response times
fix: Increase maxconn and backlog in HAProxy configuration
Raise system file descriptor limit: ulimit -n 100000
Adjust timeout values to match backend response times
Enable connection queue monitoring
prevention: Load test to determine appropriate connection limits
Monitor connection metrics and set up alerts
Implement connection rate limiting at the edge
Use connection pooling for backend servers

title: TLS certificates expiring causing service outages
symptoms: Services returning SSL certificate expired errors
Browsers blocking connections with security warnings
API clients failing to establish secure connections
Certificate monitoring alerts not firing before expiry
root_cause: Certificate renewal process not automated
Certificate authority renewal failed or delayed
Monitoring system not configured for certificate expiry
Multiple certificates with different expiry dates
diagnosis: Check certificate expiry: openssl x509 -enddate -noout -in cert.pem
Review certificate authority renewal status
Verify monitoring configuration for certificate alerts
Audit all certificates in use across infrastructure
fix: Renew expired certificate immediately with certificate authority
Implement automated certificate renewal with Let's Encrypt certbot
Update load balancer and service configurations with new certificates
Set up monitoring to alert 30 days before expiry
prevention: Automate certificate renewal with ACME protocol
Implement certificate monitoring with alerts
Use certificate management tools like cert-manager
Document certificate renewal procedures

title: iptables rules blocking legitimate traffic
symptoms: Applications cannot communicate despite network connectivity
Services accessible from some hosts but not others
Connection timeouts to specific ports or protocols
Issue resolved after iptables flush but returns after reboot
root_cause: Overly restrictive firewall rules blocking required traffic
Rule order causing legitimate traffic to be dropped by default deny
NAT or masquerading rules misconfigured
Conflicting rules from different management tools
diagnosis: List current rules: iptables -L -v -n
Check rule order and counters
Test with specific rules: iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
Review firewall configuration files
fix: Identify and remove overly restrictive rules
Add explicit allow rules for required traffic before deny rules
Fix NAT or masquerading configuration
Use firewall management tools like firewalld or UFW for consistency
prevention: Document firewall rules and their purpose
Use version control for firewall configurations
Test firewall changes in staging environment
Implement firewall rule validation scripts

title: VPN tunnel dropping frequently causing connectivity issues
symptoms: Inter-site connectivity intermittent
Applications losing connection to remote services
VPN logs showing tunnel re-establishment events
Performance degraded when tunnel is unstable
root_cause: Network instability or packet loss between endpoints
VPN keepalive or dead peer detection interval too short
NAT traversal issues with firewall or NAT devices
MTU mismatch causing packet fragmentation issues
diagnosis: Check VPN logs for tunnel establishment and teardown events
Monitor network connectivity with ping and traceroute
Review keepalive and DPD settings
Check for MTU issues: ping -M do -s 1472 <remote-host>
fix: Increase keepalive and DPD intervals to tolerate network instability
Configure NAT traversal settings for firewall compatibility
Adjust MTU settings to match network path
Implement tunnel monitoring and automatic recovery
prevention: Use stable network infrastructure for VPN endpoints
Configure appropriate keepalive settings for network conditions
Implement redundant VPN tunnels for high availability
Monitor VPN tunnel health and set up alerts

title: Load balancer introducing latency to client requests
symptoms: Client response times higher when going through load balancer
Direct access to backend servers shows normal performance
Load balancer CPU or memory usage high
Connection establishment time increased
root_cause: Load balancer under-provisioned for traffic volume
SSL/TLS termination consuming significant resources
Inefficient load balancing algorithm causing uneven distribution
Health checks too frequent adding overhead
diagnosis: Monitor load balancer resource usage
Measure request latency through load balancer vs direct
Review load balancing algorithm configuration
Check health check frequency and impact
fix: Scale up load balancer resources or add more instances
Offload SSL/TLS to dedicated hardware or use passthrough
Optimize load balancing algorithm for workload
Adjust health check intervals to reduce overhead
prevention: Right-size load balancer based on expected traffic
Use connection pooling and keep-alive settings
Implement load balancer monitoring and auto-scaling
Regularly performance test load balancer capacity

title: Network congestion causing packet loss and retransmissions
symptoms: Applications experiencing slow or intermittent connectivity
Network monitoring showing high packet loss rates
TCP retransmissions increasing during peak hours
Bandwidth utilization consistently near capacity
root_cause: Network bandwidth insufficient for traffic volume
Network device or link capacity exceeded
Broadcast storms or network loops causing congestion
Misconfigured QoS causing priority traffic to be dropped
diagnosis: Monitor bandwidth utilization with tools like iftop or nload
Check for packet loss: ping -c 100 <host> and mtr
Review switch/router interface statistics
Analyze network traffic patterns with NetFlow
fix: Upgrade network bandwidth or add additional links
Implement traffic shaping and QoS policies
Identify and eliminate broadcast storms or network loops
Optimize application traffic patterns and protocols
prevention: Monitor network capacity and plan for growth
Implement network segmentation to isolate traffic
Use QoS to prioritize critical traffic
Regularly audit network configuration and performance

title: DNS changes not propagating causing resolution failures
symptoms: New DNS records not resolving for some clients
DNS queries returning old IP addresses after changes
Some users can access services while others cannot
DNS changes take longer than expected to propagate
root_cause: DNS TTL values too high causing long caching
DNS resolvers not honoring TTL and caching longer
DNS zone not properly delegated to authoritative servers
DNS change not properly propagated to all authoritative servers
diagnosis: Check DNS record with multiple resolvers: dig <domain> @8.8.8.8
Review DNS TTL values: dig <domain> +noall +answer
Verify DNS delegation: dig NS <domain>
Check authoritative server responses
fix: Lower TTL values before making DNS changes
Verify DNS changes propagated to all authoritative servers
Flush DNS caches on local resolvers if needed
Use DNS monitoring tools to track propagation
prevention: Plan DNS changes with appropriate TTL lowering in advance
Use DNS monitoring to track propagation status
Implement DNS change management procedures
Consider using DNS providers with fast propagation

title: SOCKS proxy connections timing out for certain destinations
symptoms: Applications through proxy timeout for specific destinations
Direct connections to same destinations work fine
Proxy logs show connection attempts but no responses
Issue affects only certain protocols or ports
root_cause: Proxy access control rules blocking specific destinations
Proxy timeout settings too short for slow destinations
DNS resolution issues within proxy infrastructure
Proxy server resource limitations
diagnosis: Check proxy logs for connection attempts and denials
Test direct connectivity to affected destinations
Review proxy access control configuration
Monitor proxy server resource usage
fix: Update proxy access control rules to allow required destinations
Increase proxy timeout values for slow responding services
Fix DNS configuration on proxy server
Scale proxy resources or add additional proxy servers
prevention: Document proxy access control requirements
Implement proxy monitoring and alerting
Test proxy configuration with all required destinations
Use proxy auto-configuration for client management
